creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Looking for things to do around Florida? Comment "Guide" and I'll send you my interactive Florida map and guide with over 1,000 spots to explore! I did the work for you so you can just hit the road without hours of research and stress!

Boca Grande is one of the CUTEST beach towns in Florida!

Just two hours south of Tampa on Florida’s southwest coast lies Gasparilla Island where you will find this quaint island beach town. 

Have you ever been?

Check out:

 1. Abandoned train tracks running over the shallow water as you cross the Boca Grande Causeway
 2. Johann Fust Community Library (trust me!)
 3. Newlin’s Gourmet for a lobster roll (legendary!)
 4. Gasparilla Inn and Club (old Florida charm and big bucks to stay!) 
 5. Gasparilla Island Lighthouse 
 6. Our Lady of Mercy Catholic Church
 7. Whidden Marina
 8. Pink Elephant (good food and drinks with a cute pink elephant charm on every drink!) 

#bocagrandeflorida #uniqueflorida #floridabeachtowns #floridahiddengems #gasparillaisland

transcription.text: Come with me to explore the cutest beach town in Florida. Welcome to Boca Grande located on Gasparilla Island on the west coast about two hours south of Tampa. To get onto the island a six dollar bridge toll is required to cross the Boca Grande Causeway. Then look to the left and check out the abandoned railroad tracks running over the water. Your first stop will be the Johann Fuchs Library. Trust me on this one, this is a must stop and you're sure to love it. Grab lunch at Newland's Gourmet. Their lobster roll is legendary and a local's favorite. Check out the historic and very expensive Gasparilla Inn and Club. Even if you don't stay here you can explore the hotel and grounds. Your next stop will be the Gasparilla Island Lighthouse built in 1881 and yes you can climb it. Then visit the Our Lady of Mercy Catholic Church. The architecture and serenity are breathtaking. Next go see Wooden Marina founded in 1925 and still run by the Wooden family today. Grab dinner and drinks at the Pink Elephant. I love the pink and green decor here. Be sure to save this post and send it to a friend you would want to visit here with and follow for more Florida content from a local.
